member.name: Freddie van Mierlo
member.synopsis: Freddie van Mierlo is the Liberal Democrat MP for Henley and Thame , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when he plans to respond to the letter of 2nd June 2026 from the Hon. Member for Henley and Thame.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I replied to the Hon. Member’s correspondence of 2 June about the NHS Thames Valley Integrated Care Board on 9 July 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of maternity services not recruiting newly qualified midwives on patient safety, continuity of care and overall costs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are aware of the difficulties newly qualified midwives are experiencing in securing employment in the National Health Service. NHS England actively monitors future nursing and midwifery student numbers, current vacancies, and staff turnover and is taki...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate he has made of the number of newly qualified midwives in England in 2026 unable to secure employment within NHS maternity services; and whether he plans to introduce a nationally fund
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are aware of the difficulties newly qualified midwives are experiencing in securing employment in the National Health Service. NHS England actively monitors future nursing and midwifery student numbers, current vacancies, and staff turnover and is taki...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Disabled Facilities Grants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of whether funding provided to local authorities for Disabled Facilities Grants (DFG) is adequate for them to meet the cost of adaptations, in the context of increases i
writtenQuestions.answerText: In England, we continue to fund the Disabled Facilities Grant (DFG) which is delivered by local authorities. This grant helps eligible older and disabled people on low incomes to adapt their homes to make them safe and suitable for their needs.We have con...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Culture and Sports: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to ensure adequate opportunities for under 16s to engage in sport and cultural activities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that everyone, including children and young people, have access to and benefit from quality sport and cultural opportunities.Our arm’s length body Sport England works to increase participation in grassroots sport, i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Clubs: Young People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to ensure adequate opportunities for under 16s to engage in joint social activities with other young people.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We’re working closely across government to expand access to real‑world activities as we know that strong offline connections between young people are essential to supporting their wellbeing and their development. In December 2025, DCMS published ‘Youth Ma...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Individual Savings Accounts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to Question 7286 answered on 15 June 2026, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of the metric used to assess the £450,000 Lifetime Isa limit for first time buyers; and whether she has made an estima
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK House Price Index is an official statistic produced jointly by HM Land Registry, the Office for National Statistics and other government departments. Information on the methodology used to calculate house prices and averages is available at: About ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Pregnancy: Streptococcus
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when the UK National Screening Committee is scheduled to review the evidence from the GBS3 trial, to consider whether screening for Group B Streptococcus should be routinely offered during pregnanc
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK National Screening Committee is in close contact with the GBS3 trial researchers and stands ready to commence a review of the evidence for antenatal screening for Group B Streptococcus as soon as the trial reports.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Strategic Lawsuits against Public Participation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, pursuant to answer of 10 June 2026 to question 3175, if he will take steps to have those discussions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no current plans to have discussions with My Rt Hon. Friend, the Secretary of State for Justice on protecting maternity safety campaigners from Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Cuckooing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what timeframe her Department has set for publishing statutory guidance to police forces on the offence of cuckooing under the Crime and Policing Act 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Crime and Policing Act introduces a new offence of ‘cuckooing’ (controlling another’s home for criminal purposes). The new cuckooing offence will ensure that we are taking strong action against those who prey on vulnerable people and help to ensure th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Teesside Freeport: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the value for money to the taxpayer of the arrangements between the South Tees Development Corporation and Teesworks Ltd in relation to the acquisition,
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of securing value for money for taxpayers and is committed to making the UK a world leader in AI, attracting private investment, supporting growth and creating high-quality jobs.Commercial arrangements relating to ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Universal Credit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps she is taking to ensure that Universal Credit claimants can obtain clear and consistent guidance on whether particular financial receipts including the sale of personal possessions must be de
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not routinely require receipts for the sale or disposal of personal possessions. Claimants must declare capital that may affect entitlement to means-tested benefits and are continually advised and reminded of their responsibility for d...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Thames Water: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what contingency planning her Department has undertaken to monitor and improve Thames Water’s Asset Health Index in future.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In line with Section 2 duties of the Water Industry Act 1991, the Secretary of State has given her preliminary views on the London and Valley Water Consortium’s Proposal to Ofwat, but it is for Ofwat to decide whether to reject or accept the Proposal. The...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, with reference to her letter of Monday 15th June 2026 to Ofwat on Thames Water’s performance and infrastructure, what assessment she has made of the potential implications for her Depa
writtenQuestions.answerText: In line with Section 2 duties of the Water Industry Act 1991, the Secretary of State has given her preliminary views on the London and Valley Water Consortium’s Proposal to Ofwat, but it is for Ofwat to decide whether to reject or accept the Proposal. The...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Thames Water
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, pursuant to question asked by the hon. member for Henley and Thame during a statement to the house on 16th June by the Secretary of State on Thames Water, if she will publish outputs o
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is preparing for all eventualities including special administration. The work done by FTI would not be appropriate to make public. It would also not be appropriate at this time to make public the current spending on contingency planning.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurodiversity: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate he has made of a) the number of children and adolescents who have been waiting for more than 5 years for assessment by neurodevelopmental diagnostic services and b) the effectiveness
writtenQuestions.answerText: Published data on Autism Waiting times does not include figures on the number of people waiting over five years for an assessment. However, there is data based on how long those who are being seen following a referral for an assessment for autism have wai...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: House of Commons: Absent Voting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the hon. Member for Blaenau Gwent and Rhymney, representing the House of Commons Commission, whether the Commission has made an assessment of the potential merits of implementing remote digital voting in the House of Commons.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Introducing remote digital voting would be a matter for the House itself and not for the Commission. Remote voting was used for a short period during the pandemic but was replaced by a system of proxies and pass reader voting. The hon. Member could raise ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: House of Commons Commission

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he will take to ensure that (a) quality and (b) safety of maternity services are more (i) clearly and (ii) consistently reflected in CQC ratings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Care Quality Commission (CQC) is committed to strengthening its oversight of maternity services and has commissioned an independent developmental review to identify opportunities to improve how quality and safety are assessed and reflected in ratings,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Project Gigabit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of Project Gigabit contract redesign on the roll out of gigabit broadband.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to achieving nationwide gigabit broadband coverage by 2032. We are making good progress on Project Gigabit, and as of the end of March 2026, over 1.4 million premises in hard-to-reach communities across the UK had been upgrade...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Reviews
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will make it his policy to produce a set of specific local recommendations for each trust included in the Amos review of maternity and neonatal care following the publication of the National
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation, led by Baroness Amos, is bringing together the findings from past reviews, local investigations in selected trusts, and evidence from families and staff into one single set of national recommendations.The...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
